mnemonics: SOAP vs REST: SOAP is Stateful, One-way, and Protocol-heavy, while REST is Resource-oriented, Stateless, and Transfer-focused.
keyPoints: IoT architecture typically consists of three or four layers: Perception/Sensing, Network, Processing/Middleware, and Application.
A key differentiator between architectures is how data is processed; some models use centralized clouds while others leverage edge or fog computing closer to the devices.
Communication protocols (like MQTT, CoAP, HTTP) are a fundamental architectural choice, impacting efficiency, reliability, and power consumption.
commonMistakes: Assuming all IoT architectures are the same and can be applied universally, without considering specific use case requirements like latency, bandwidth, or security.
Confusing the roles of Edge and Fog computing; Edge is on the local device/network, while Fog is an intermediate layer between Edge and the Cloud.
